openFindings.title: GEMINI.md routes to the deleted CocoIndex MCP tool
openFindings.description: Replace the SEARCH ROUTING line (`use CocoIndex semantic search (mcp__cocoindex_code__search)`) with the HYBRID policy (Code Graph + Grep) to match the other four runtime routing docs. A Gemini session would be told to call a non-existent MCP tool.

openFindings.title: advisor database/skill-graph.json carries 8 stale system-rerank-sidecar / mcp-coco-index / 8765 references
openFindings.description: Lines 37/144/221-228/232/239/493-497/522 (system list, siblings, adjacency block, depends_on, enhances, keywords incl localhost 8765 rerank, hub_skills). Regenerate the database/ copy from the recompiled scripts/ skill-graph.json so the advisor never routes to deleted skills.

openFindings.title: /memory:manage declares the removed ccc subcommand + CCC MODE section
openFindings.description: Lines 3/19/25/40/168/944 (argument-hint, mode list, error message, action routing, router, CCC MODE §16). Remove the ccc subcommand + CCC MODE §16-17 (the ccc_* tools were removed in phase 002).

openFindings.title: spec.md phase-map claims phases 002-012 complete despite residue
openFindings.description: The phase-map table marks all phases 002-012 as "Complete", but iter-1 found 4 misses (F001-F004: GEMINI.md routing, .gitignore, skill-graph.json, /memory:manage) and iter-2 found 2 P2s (F005-F006: embedder_pluggability obsolete commands, doctor_deep-loop.yaml vestigial glob) that should have been cleaned in those phases. Either update the phase-map to reflect residue status or add a "complete with known residue" qualifier to maintain traceability accuracy.

openFindings.title: Dead RERANK_ env var prefix in sidecar allowlist for removed feature
openFindings.description: The `RERANK_` prefix is still allowed in the sidecar env allowlist, but `RERANKER_LOCAL` was removed in the 014 deprecation (system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:422 states "the SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER/RERANKER_LOCAL flags are no longer wired"). Allowing env vars for a dead feature is a security/maintainability risk. Recommendation: Remove `'RERANK_'` from the prefixes array in SIDECAR_ENV_ALLOWLIST.

openFindings.title: Stale feature catalog claims deleted `07--ccc-integration` directory
openFindings.description: The catalog claims 3 features in `07--ccc-integration/` (lines 48, 287, 291, 307, 323) with TOC entry (line 29), but the directory does not exist. Lines 287, 291, 323 reference the deleted `ccc` binary and tools. This is analogous to F008 found in iter-6 for the code-graph catalog. Recommendation: Remove the `07--ccc-integration` section entirely (TOC entry + 3 feature entries) or replace with a deprecation note.

openFindings.title: Misleading tool handler references to deleted `lib/ccc/` paths
openFindings.description: Lines 84-86 claim tools route to `lib/ccc/` (deleted in 002), and line 115 references deleted `ccc_bridge_integration.md`. The actual handlers are `lib/scan/`, `lib/status/`, `lib/verify/` per the code-graph tree-sitter architecture. Recommendation: Update handler map to reflect actual tree-sitter paths and remove the deleted integration doc reference.

openFindings.title: Dead cross-references to deleted `ccc_bridge_integration.md`
openFindings.description: `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/README.md:343`, `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/SKILL.md:160,291,404` — Three files reference the deleted integration doc. The integration doc does not exist (`integrations/` directory is gone). Recommendation: Remove these cross-references or replace with a note about the 002 decouple.

openFindings.title: .gitignore has a stale .cocoindex_code/ ignore entry
openFindings.description: Remove the ignore entry for the deleted directory.

openFindings.title: embedder_pluggability.md §3 has obsolete ccc run-daemon command with obsolescence banner
openFindings.description: The section carries a clear "⚠️ Obsolete as of the 014 CocoIndex deprecation" banner, but the ccc-based commands and .venv/bin/ccc paths no longer exist. While this is a documented exception, the commands are misleading if someone tries to follow them. Consider removing or collapsing the obsolete system-code-graph columns entirely.

openFindings.title: doctor_deep-loop.yaml has forbidden target glob for coco databases
openFindings.description: Line 97 includes `"mcp_server/database/*coco*"` in forbidden_targets. This is defensive (prevents writes to non-existent coco databases), but the glob is vestigial since coco databases no longer exist. Remove the glob pattern as cleanup.

openFindings.title: Stale catalog entry claiming removed CCC integration capability
openFindings.description: The feature catalog references a non-existent `07--ccc-integration` directory and claims CCC integration capabilities (ccc-reindex, ccc-feedback, ccc-status tools) that were removed in the 014 deprecation. Directory listing shows no 07--ccc-integration folder exists. Recommendation: Remove the CCC integration section from the catalog or replace with a deprecation notice.

openFindings.title: Vestigial `RERANK_` env prefix in sidecar allowlist
openFindings.description: The `RERANK_` prefix (line 17) is dead since the rerank sidecar was deleted in 014. This file itself may be vestigial since no sidecar exists to consume the allowlist. Recommendation: Remove the `RERANK_` prefix and evaluate whether the entire file is still needed (no active sidecar consumers).

openFindings.title: Obsolete ccc path reference in session-start testing playbook
openFindings.description: Lines 20, 23, 143 reference `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/.venv/bin/ccc` which no longer exists (tree-sitter has no venv/ccc). Recommendation: Update to reflect tree-sitter readiness check or remove the ccc-specific assertion.
